title: Integrate Google SSO via Stytch alongside Existing Salesforce Authentication
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to sign in with my Google account, so that I can access the platform without requiring a Salesforce license or Salesforce OAuth credentials.
description:
Introduce Google SSO authentication using Stytch as a second authentication method alongside the existing Salesforce-based OAuth. This enables the platform to support multiple identity providers, improves onboarding flexibility, and prepares the system for enterprise and non-Salesforce users.

## Supported Authentication Methods

1. **Salesforce OAuth** (existing) — current production auth flow
2. **Google SSO via Stytch** (new) — new auth provider

## Requirements

- Both authentication methods securely map users to the correct tenant
- Cross-tenant access is prevented regardless of auth method
- Users authenticating via Google get a Stytch Organization and Member created automatically

## Open Questions

- **Account linking**: What happens when a user with an existing Salesforce account tries Google SSO with the same email? Options discussed:
  1. Auto-link accounts (recommended — user can use either method)
  2. Create separate accounts (duplicate user problem)
  3. Block Google login for existing Salesforce users

## Implementation

Google-authenticated customers get a Stytch Organization and Member created. The user is then authorized to use the app. Both `stytch_user_id` and `sfuid` can coexist on a user record when accounts are linked.
acceptance_criteria: Users can authenticate via Google SSO through Stytch
Google-authenticated users are mapped to the correct tenant
Stytch Organization and Member are created automatically for Google users
Cross-tenant access is prevented for Google-authenticated users
Existing Salesforce authentication continues to work
Account linking strategy for same-email users across providers is implemented
ux_changes: New 'Sign in with Google' option on the login page alongside the existing Salesforce OAuth button.
middleware_changes: Google SSO authentication flow via Stytch: handles OAuth callback, creates Stytch Organization and Member, maps user to tenant, and establishes session.
data_model_changes: User records support dual auth provider fields (stytch_user_id alongside existing sfuid) for account linking.
api_changes: Google OAuth callback endpoint for Stytch authentication flow.
implementation_notes: The account linking question is the key design decision. Auto-linking (option 1) provides the best UX — users with existing Salesforce accounts can seamlessly start using Google SSO by matching on email. Both stytch_user_id and sfuid coexist on the user record. The implementation creates a Stytch Organization and Member on first Google auth, then authorizes the user for app access.
comment_summary: Developer asked for clarification on account linking strategy when same-email users exist across Salesforce and Google SSO. Implementation complete: Google-authenticated users get a Stytch Organization and Member created automatically, then are authorized for app access.
